name: Winston
what_it_does: Multi-transport async logging library for Node.js. Winston's primary strength is its transport system — route logs to multiple destinations simultaneously (console, files, HTTP, databases) with configurable format transforms per transport. Supports custom levels, colorized output, JSON formatting, splat interpolation, and printf-style formatting. The most flexible (if slower) Node.js logger.
use_cases: Route logs to multiple simultaneous destinations — console in development, JSON file in production, error-only to alert endpoint
Build logging infrastructure for agent systems that need to write to both local files and remote logging services
Implement structured logging with custom formats — combine timestamp, correlation IDs, and error stacks in consistent format
Use winston-daily-rotate-file for rotating log files in long-running agent servers
Integrate with external log aggregators via community transports (winston-cloudwatch, winston-elasticsearch, winston-papertrail)
not_for: High-throughput production servers where logging performance is critical — use Pino which is 5x faster
Simple single-destination logging — Pino or console.log are simpler for basic cases
Browser logging — Winston is Node.js specific
best_when: You need flexible multi-transport logging with the rich ecosystem of community transports — Winston's transport system enables routing to any destination.
avoid_when: You need maximum logging performance in a high-throughput server — Pino is significantly faster. Use Pino for performance-sensitive applications.

auth.notes: No authentication — logging library. Remote transports configure auth separately.

performance.rate_limits: No rate limits — local logging library

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Winston transport errors surface as error events on the transport — must add error handlers to each transport. Logger configuration errors at setup time are descriptive.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Logging is a side effect — idempotency not applicable.
known_agent_gotchas: Winston transport errors don't throw — they emit 'error' events on the transport instance; must add error handlers or errors are silently swallowed
Multiple logger instances across modules sharing transports can cause duplicate logging — use winston.createLogger() once and export the instance, don't create in each module
Format combiners must be in correct order — winston.format.combine(timestamp(), json()) order matters; wrong order produces malformed output
winston-daily-rotate-file is a separate package with its own versioning — ensure it's compatible with your winston version
Winston 3.x changed the API significantly from 2.x — online tutorials mixing versions cause configuration confusion
Logging Error objects requires format.errors({stack: true}) — without this format, Error instances log as empty {}

security_score_components.security_notes: Log output may contain sensitive data — implement format transforms to redact sensitive fields. Winston has no built-in redaction; implement custom formatter.
